2026-04-22T03:09:15.3904760Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3905665Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3906132Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3906496Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3906800Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3907031Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3907183Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T03:09:15.3907361Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-22T03:09:15.3907755Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T03:09:15.3908488Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"item_number":{"description":"The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.","type":["number","string"]},"reply_to_id":{"description":"Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. 2026-04-22T03:11:42.7375281Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T03:10:04.56358531Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58472,\"title\":\"[ComputeLimit] Docs: Add service-specific examples and snippets for new GA resources\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57628 by `@azure-sdk` (co-authored by `@dapzhang`, `@hebin-huang`, `@ajbhal`)\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57628 promoted `Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` from `1.0.0-beta.1` to `1.0.0` GA, adding two new resource families:\\n\\n- **`ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` / `ComputeLimitVmFamilyCollection` / `ComputeLimitVmFamilyData`** — new resource type for managing VM family resources under a subscription location\\n- **`ComputeLimitFeatureResource` / `ComputeLimitFeatureCollection` / `ComputeLimitFeatureData`** — new resource type with a `Disable` operation for managing compute limit features\\n\\nThe package enables subscriptions to share compute quota limits with guest subscriptions.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The README **Examples** section contains only a generic pointer to `(aka.ms/redacted) with no package-specific code samples\\n- There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** — no snippet-backed test files exist for any resource in this package\\n- The new `ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource` and `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` resources (added in 1.0.0) have **no usage examples** anywhere\\n- The `Disable` operation on `ComputeLimitFeatureResource` is not demonstrated\\n- The **Key Concepts** section is entirely generic — it describes no service-specific concepts (e.g., what a \\u0026#34;guest subscription\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;shared limit\\u0026#34;, \\u0026#34;VM family\\u0026#34;, or \\u0026#34;feature\\u0026#34; means in this context)\\n- The service description in the README header is a single sentence and does not describe the new resource hierarchy\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File 1:** `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\nReplace the `## Key concepts` and `## Examples` sections with service-specific content:\\n\\n```markdown\\n## Key concepts\\n\\n`Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit` enables subscriptions (called *host* subscriptions) to share their allocated Azure compute quota with other subscriptions (*guest* subscriptions). Core resource types:\\n\\n- **`ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource`** — represents a guest subscription that has been granted access to the host\\u0026#39;s compute quota.\\n- **`ComputeLimitSharedLimitResource`** — represents a shared quota limit configuration between a host and its guests.\\n- **`ComputeLimitFeatureResource`** — represents an optional compute limit feature that can be enabled or disabled on a subscription.\\n- **`ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource`** — represents a VM family entry (e.g., `standardDSv3Family`) within a subscription-location scope, used to track or adjust per-family limits.\\n\\n### Thread safety\\n\\nAll client instance methods are thread-safe. All resource objects are immutable; operations return new instances.\\n\\n### Additional concepts\\n\\n[Client options](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#configuring-service-clients-using-clientoptions) |\\n[Accessing the response](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#accessing-http-response-details-using-responset) |\\n[Long-running operations](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#consuming-long-running-operations-using-operationt) |\\n[Handling failures](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#reporting-errors-requestfailedexception) |\\n[Diagnostics](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#logging) |\\n[Mocking](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md#mocking) |\\n[Client lifetime]((devblogs.microsoft.com/redacted)\\n\\n## Examples\\n\\nThe following sections show several code snippets illustrating common patterns for this library.\\n\\n### List VM families for a subscription location\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Get and disable a compute limit feature\\n\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### List guest subscriptions sharing compute quota\\n\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n**File 2:** `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/tests/Samples/Sample1_VmFamilies.cs` — **create**\\n\\n```csharp\\n// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\\n\\nusing System;\\nusing System.Threading.Tasks;\\nusing Azure.Identity;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit;\\nusing Azure.ResourceManager.Resources;\\nusing NUnit.Framework;\\n\\nnamespace Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit.Tests.Samples\\n{\\n    public class Sample1_VmFamilies\\n    {\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListVmFamilies()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimitSample_ListVmFamilies\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // List all VM family resources in a specific location\\n            string location = \\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;;\\n            await foreach (ComputeLimitVmFamilyResource vmFamily in subscription.GetComputeLimitVmFamiliesAsync(location))\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;VM Family: {vmFamily.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task DisableFeature()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimitSample_DisableFeature\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // Get a specific compute limit feature and disable it\\n            string location = \\u0026#34;eastus\\u0026#34;;\\n            string featureName = \\u0026#34;SharedQuota\\u0026#34;;\\n            ComputeLimitFeatureResource feature = await subscription\\n                .GetComputeLimitFeatureAsync(location, featureName);\\n\\n            // Disable the feature\\n            await feature.DisableAsync(WaitUntil.Completed);\\n            Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Feature \\u0026#39;{featureName}\\u0026#39; has been disabled.\\u0026#34;);\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n\\n        [Test]\\n        [Ignore(\\u0026#34;Only verifying that the sample builds\\u0026#34;)]\\n        public async Task ListGuestSubscriptions()\\n        {\\n            #region Snippet:ComputeLimitSample_ListGuestSubscriptions\\n            ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential());\\n            SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync();\\n\\n            // List all guest subscriptions that share quota with this subscription\\n            await foreach (ComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionResource guestSub in subscription.GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionsAsync())\\n            {\\n                Console.WriteLine($\\u0026#34;Guest subscription: {guestSub.Data.Name}\\u0026#34;);\\n            }\\n            #endregion\\n        }\\n    }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nThe three snippet regions defined in the test file above map to the README placeholders as follows:\\n\\n| Snippet Name | Test method | README location |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `ComputeLimitSample_ListVmFamilies` | `ListVmFamilies()` | Under `### List VM families for a subscription location` |\\n| `ComputeLimitSample_DisableFeature` | `DisableFeature()` | Under `### Get and disable a compute limit feature` |\\n| `ComputeLimitSample_ListGuestSubscriptions` | `ListGuestSubscriptions()` | Under `### List guest subscriptions sharing compute quota` |\\n\\nEach README placeholder block is already included in the Step 1 README edits above.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/README.md` currently **has** all required top-level sections. The following need content improvements:\\n\\n| Section | Status | Action |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| Getting started → Install the package | ✅ Complete | — |\\n| Getting started → Prerequisites | ✅ Complete | — |\\n| Getting started → Authenticate the client | ✅ Complete | — |\\n| Key concepts | ❌ Generic boilerplate | Replace with service-specific content (see Step 1) |\\n| Examples | ❌ No code samples | Add snippet placeholders + test file (see Steps 1–2) |\\n| Troubleshooting | ✅ Complete | — |\\n| Next steps | ✅ Complete | — |\\n| Contributing | ✅ Complete | — |\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next.\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 computelimit` — injects snippet code into README placeholder blocks; verify with `git diff` that README was updated\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 computelimit` — regenerates API listing files (no public API changes expected, but run to confirm)\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/computelimit/Azure.ResourceManager.ComputeLimit/` — ensures consistent formatting\\n\\n\\u0026gt; **Note:** The exact method signatures for `GetComputeLimitVmFamiliesAsync`, `GetComputeLimitFeatureAsync`, `DisableAsync`, and `GetComputeLimitGuestSubscriptionsAsync` should be verified against the generated extension methods in `src/Generated/Extensions/ComputeLimitExtensions.cs` and the resource files before writing the samples. Adjust parameter names/types as needed to match the actual generated API.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!WARNING]\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\u0026gt; \\u003cstrong\\u003e⚠️ Firewall blocked 1 domain\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; The following domain was blocked by the firewall during workflow execution:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; network:\\n\\u0026gt;   allowed:\\n\\u0026gt;     - defaults\\n\\u0026gt;     - \\u0026#34;centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com\\u0026#34;\\n\\u0026gt; ```\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information.\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; \\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24757913738/agentic_workflow) · ● 533.1K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22\\u0026amp;type=issues)\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\",\"state\":\"open\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58472\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"github-actions[bot]\",\"id\":41898282,\"profile_url\":\"https://github.com/apps/github-actions\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4\"},\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"reactions\":{\"total_count\":0,\"+1\":0,\"-1\":0,\"laugh\":0,\"confused\":0,\"heart\":0,\"hooray\":0,\"rocket\":0,\"eyes\":0},\"created_at\":\"2026-04-22T03:07:41Z\",\"updated_at\":\"2026-04-22T03:07:41Z\"}","type":"text"}]}}}
